They loved this country, proud of its accomplishments and respect given it worldwide. We’re all Canadians, from the Atlantic to our

articles.title: JAY GOLDBERG: Bill C-22 threatens your privacy – and your wallet
articles.description: Canadians will literally be footing the bill for the government to require companies to track their comings and goings, as well as their communications, and keep that information for an entire year.

articles.title: Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
articles.description: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

articles.title: Carney heading for Ireland, France to deepen ties and attend G7 summit
articles.description: The summit is running from June 15 to June 17 in Evian-les-Bains and France says the focus will be on reducing global inequalities.

articles.title: Peru’s discontented voters face straight left-right choice in election runoff

articles.title: Scientists finally complete Schrödinger’s 100-year-old color theory
articles.description: Researchers have finally resolved a key problem in a 100-year-old theory of color, showing that the qualities we perceive in colors are intrinsic to the mathematics of color space itself. articles.title: Tiny X-ray telescope could unlock the Moon's hidden chemistry
articles.description: A lightweight new X-ray telescope could finally give scientists something they’ve never had before: a complete chemical map of the Moon. Researchers used detailed mission simulations to show that a compact telescope orbiting the Moon could identify key elements across the entire lunar surface, helpi

articles.title: Scientists found a surprisingly simple way to create powerful quantum states
articles.description: A team at the University of Chicago has discovered a surprisingly simple way to create powerful quantum states that are normally difficult to produce. articles.title: Ebola spread in central Africa could match 2014 record outbreak, US health officials say
articles.description: Modelling from US CDC shows Ebola spread could be on ‘dangerous trajectory’, but experts warn outbreaks can be very hard to predictCentral Africa’s Ebola outbreak could spread to be similar in scale to the worst outbreak in history, west Africa’s 2014-2016 outbreak that killed more than 11,000 peopl

articles.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
articles.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

articles.title: House of Commons passes bill criminalizing forced sterilization
articles.description: The House of Commons on Monday passed a long-awaited law criminalizing forced or coerced sterilization,... The post House of Commons passes bill criminalizing forced sterilization appeared first on APTN News.

articles.title: Planet nine mystery deepens as new discovery challenges hidden planet theory
articles.description: Astronomers have spent years searching for a possible hidden giant planet far beyond Neptune. Unusual orbits among distant Kuiper Belt objects have fueled the Planet Nine theory, but recent discoveries are challenging the idea by showing more stable motion than expected.
